sections.paragraph:
συναγωγή, ῆς, ἡ [συνάγω (reduplicated form of ἄγω)]

—1. ‘place of assembly’, in NT specifically of a local Israelite cultic and community center, which in the early decades of the Messianic movement would be used also by followers of Jesus: transliterated synagogue Mt 4:23; 10:17; Mk 1:39; Lk 4:15f; 7:5; J 6:59; 18:20; Ac 13:14; 17:17; 18:4; 22:19.

—2. ‘synagogal group’,assembly, synagogue Ac 6:9; 9:2. In polemical context Rv 2:9; 3:9.

—3. ‘synagogal meeting’, meeting: of Judeans Ac 13:43; of Judeo-Christians Js 2:2.
